fields.summary: Potential infinite redirection on YARN log redirection web service

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description:
In YARN-6113, we have added a re-direct NM web service to get container logs which could cause the potential infinite redirection.

It can happens when:
* Call AHS web service to get a running/finished AM container log for a running application.
* AHS web service would re-direct the request the specific NM given the application is still running. And the NM would handle the request
* If the log file we requested has already been aggregated and deleted from NM, the NM would re-direct the request back to AHS.
In this case, we would do step 2 and step 3 infinite times.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

The testcase failure is not related

bq. btw, use "redirected" may be better.
Can we instead be more specific and say "redirected_from_node"?

bq. So if NM address is provided by user in the beginning, it sounds like we will still redirect the request to NM back and forth?
Check offline with Xuan. This is not an issue because the NM return will always drop NM address. So we are good for this issue.

Latest patch LGTM. +1 pending on Jenkins report.
